task: Write a Python function median(nums) returning the middle value of the sorted list when the length is odd, or the average of the two middle values as a float when the length is even. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code accurately calculates the median of a list and handles edge cases correctly.
trace: The function `median` first checks if the input list is empty and returns `None` in that case, handling an edge case. It then sorts the list and calculates the middle index. If the length of the list is even, it computes the average of the two middle numbers; if odd, it returns the middle number directly. The solution ensures clarity and conciseness while correctly addressing the task requirements.
